[![Modular elliptic curves and Fermat’s Last Theorem. width=320&height=427&fit=bounds&auto=webp&v=1746019876)](https://sophiararebooks.cdn.bibliopolis.com/pictures/6446a.jpg? auto=webp&v=1746019876 "Modular elliptic curves and Fermat’s Last Theorem. It seemed that the solutions for any one of the infinite number of elliptic curves could be derived from one of the infinite number of modular forms. He was unable to prove the Shimura-Taniyama conjecture, but he was able to link it with Fermat’s Last Theorem. If somebody could prove the Shimura-Taniyama conjecture, then this would immediately imply the proof of Fermat’s Last Theorem. “The Shimura-Taniyama conjecture had remained unproven since the 1950s and so there was little optimism that it was a realistic route to a proof of Fermat’s Last Theorem. “The number of elliptic curves and modular forms is infinite, and the Shimura-Taniyama conjecture claimed each elliptic curve could be matched with a modular.
